mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, weak labor market conditions signal deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-movement cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 230.0, accelerating inflation expectations force monetary tightening fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or inflation spikes do not correlate with equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate falls confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal generates negative 60-day returns in over 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms macroeconomic expansion, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant after controlling for market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production signal improving supply-side momentum and corporate output, consistently generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.
